steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage says that because of this opacity, "data-privacy advocates are seeking to expand online players' knowledge" of what data games collect. The blank describes how the games handle information about their data practices.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean unclear or non-transparent about data collection. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — opaque: not transparent, hard to understand — games that hide what data they collect are opaque, which is why advocates must educate players. ✓
  • Choice B — ambivalent: having mixed feelings — this describes an emotional attitude, not a quality of information disclosure. ✗
  • Choice C — abrasive: harsh or irritating in manner — this is about personality, not how clearly data practices are disclosed. ✗
  • Choice D — outspoken: candid and forthright — this is the opposite of hiding information; outspoken games would have no transparency problem. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
